citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care.
citations.narrative:
The facility failed to communicate with the attending veterinarian in a timely manner concerning an adverse event
involving the health and well-being of animals. Despite regular maintenance of the HVAC system in the bat housing area,
the system failed to obey the thermostat settings on 01/03/2026, leading to overheating in bat holding rooms. This
temperature excursion impacted most of the bats in the colony and resulted in the deaths of 2 female and 2 male adult
Seba's short-tailed bats and one adult male Jamaican fruit bat. Facility records and IACUC minutes document that
husbandry and laboratory staff responded immediately to mitigate the problem when they discovered the issue during
daily observations: They quickly relocated the surviving bats from the affected rooms, assessed their health and well-
being, and provided them with additional water and food to help minimize the impact of the excessive heat. The animal
facility manager and other staff were notified promptly; however, no one notified the attending veterinarian about the event
for several days. Once notified, the veterinarian agreed with the actions taken by the staff following the incident. The
facility is in the process of revising the current Contingency Plan and other documents to better emphasize timely
reporting of animal impacts to the attending veterinarian and re-training all applicable staff accordingly.
Although the attending veterinarian was finally made aware and went to the facility to evaluate the animals many days
after the event, the facility’s failure to relay the incident details to her in a timely manner prevented the veterinarian from
participating in the real-time decisions made and actions taken to help the surviving animals. The registrant must not only
ensure that animals are observed at least daily to assess their health and well-being, but also establish and maintain a
mechanism of frequent and direct communication so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health,
behavior and well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian.
TO BE CORRECTED BY 04/01/2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.33(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care. 
The facility failed to communicate with the attending veterinarian in a timely manner concerning an adverse event 
involving the health and well-being of animals. Despite regular maintenance of the HVAC system in the bat housing area, 
the system failed to obey the thermostat settings on 01/03/2026, leading to overheating in bat holding rooms. This

husbandry and laboratory staff responded immediately to mitigate the problem when they discovered the issue during 
daily observations: They quickly relocated the surviving bats from the affected rooms, assessed their health and well-
being, and provided them with additional water and food to help minimize the impact of the excessive heat. The animal 
facility manager and other staff were notified promptly; however, no one notified the attending veterinarian about the event 
for several days. Once notified, the veterinarian agreed with the actions taken by the staff following the incident. The 
facility is in the process of revising the current Contingency Plan and other documents to better emphasize timely 
reporting of animal impacts to the attending veterinarian and re-training all applicable staff accordingly. 
Although the attending veterinarian was finally made aware and went to the facility to evaluate the animals many days 
after the event, the facility’s failure to relay the incident details to her in a timely manner prevented the veterinarian from 
participating in the real-time decisions made and actions taken to help the surviving animals. The registrant must not only 
ensure that animals are observed at least daily to assess their health and well-being, but also establish and maintain a 
mechanism of frequent and direct communication so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, 
behavior and well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian. 
TO BE CORRECTED BY 04/01/2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
